sections.title: National Overview
sections.body: For the week of April 06, 2026, the Undercurrent national composite stands at 51 out of 100, based on behavioral signals from 50 US metros. Over the past month, the index has improved by 0.2 points. On a quarterly basis, 2026-Q2 is tracking up 0.9 points from 2026-Q1 (from 50 to 51). 26 of 50 metros are above the neutral 50 threshold, while 24 show sub-neutral behavioral sentiment.

sections.title: Top Movers
sections.body: The biggest gainer this week is Memphis, TN (+2 to 49). The steepest decline came from Portland, OR (-4 to 38). Other notable gainers: Memphis (+2), San Antonio (+1), Austin (+1). Other notable decliners: Portland (-4), Denver (-3), Philadelphia (-2).

sections.title: Signal Analysis
sections.body: The primary drag on sentiment this period is search anxiety, pulling the index down by a combined -1.8 points. Offsetting this, new business applications and AI job search activity contributed positively (+1.3 pts).

sections.title: Metro Spotlight
sections.body: Leading the rankings is Hartford, CT with a score of 65, while Portland, OR sits at the bottom with 38.

sections.title: Affordability
sections.body: On affordability, El Paso remains the most affordable tracked metro (score 100, 17% housing burden), while San Jose is the least affordable (score 0, 96% of income to housing).
